_meta.how_to_regress: npm run check:golden waiting-mode-liberator  (dev backend up)
_meta.note: liberate guards precomputed-alarm echo + clean type enum; review guards the total_sessions guard fix.

input.userTasks: reply to emails, walk the dog

output.free_until: You have a clear, uninterrupted stretch from 10:00 AM until your prep alarm at 1:55 PM — that's 115 minutes of genuine free time.
output.permission: You have a solid 115-minute window across two easy blocks before your dentist prep alarm fires at 1:55 PM, and both of your tasks fit cleanly inside it — you are not too blocked to begin, you just needed the map.

time_blocks.task: Walk the dog — head out now for a 25–30 minute loop.
time_blocks.why_it_fits: Light physical activity is a perfect match for moderate energy first thing, and getting it done early removes it from your mental load before the day builds.

time_blocks.task: Reply to emails — work through your inbox, prioritizing anything that needs a response today.
time_blocks.why_it_fits: You're warmed up from the walk and functional-but-not-sharp energy is exactly right for composed, thoughtful replies — no deep focus required.

output.reframe: The dentist appointment isn't eating your day — it's bookending a clean, contained morning where both your tasks fit with room to spare.

steps: Stop what you're doing when the 1:55 PM alarm fires — no finishing one more thing.
Use the restroom, grab water or a snack if needed, and collect anything you need to bring.
Head out the door so travel time is comfortable, not rushed.

output.worst_case: If the walk runs long or emails take over, drop back to just the highest-priority replies — the dentist alarm at 1:55 PM is your hard stop and everything else can wait until after you're home.

trigger_patterns.worst_trigger: Work appointments cause the most paralysis, with anxiety spiking to 8 and only 1 of 2 blocks completed despite moderate energy.
trigger_patterns.easiest: Social appointments are handled best, with the highest block completion rate (3 of 4) despite the lowest energy level.
trigger_patterns.observation: Anxiety level appears more tied to appointment type than to how much free time is available beforehand.

time_insights.avg_free_time: Average free time before appointments is about 137 minutes across all three sessions.
time_insights.utilization: Time was used 'mostly' in two sessions and only 'some' in the work session, suggesting partial engagement is the norm rather than full immersion.
time_insights.best_block_length: Sessions with more free time (200 minutes) yielded the best block completion ratio, suggesting longer unstructured windows support more momentum.

energy_patterns.avg_energy: Average energy across sessions is 3 out of what appears to be a 5-point scale, indicating consistently low-to-moderate energy in waiting mode.
energy_patterns.observation: The lowest energy session (2) still produced the best block completion, suggesting energy level alone does not predict productivity during waiting mode.

anxiety_trends.avg_anxiety_before: Average pre-appointment anxiety is 6.7 out of 10, indicating meaningful anticipatory distress across all session types.
anxiety_trends.avg_reality: All three sessions resolved as 'fine' or 'better than feared,' meaning reality was never as bad as anticipated.
anxiety_trends.gap: There is a consistent and significant gap between anticipated dread and actual outcome — fear reliably overstates the difficulty.
anxiety_trends.insight: Work appointments showed the starkest mismatch: anxiety hit 8 but reality landed at 'better than feared,' making work the type most worth targeting with pre-reframe strategies.

recommendations.insight: Work appointments trigger high anxiety but consistently resolve better than feared, yet they produce the lowest block completion.
recommendations.suggestion: Before work appointments, try a single written sentence — 'Last time this felt like an 8 and turned out better than feared' — to interrupt the freeze before it starts.

recommendations.insight: Block completion is higher when more free time is available, but even partial completion ('some' usage) still represents meaningful reclaimed time.
recommendations.suggestion: Reframe partial completion as a win by setting a visible minimum of just one block rather than aiming for the full plan.

recommendations.insight: Energy is consistently low during waiting mode across all session types.
recommendations.suggestion: Pre-select one low-demand 'anchor task' the night before any appointment so there is zero decision cost when energy is already depleted.

output.encouragement: Across every single session, reality was fine or better than feared — your anxiety is a loud forecaster that has yet to be right, and your data proves it.
